name: Andy Razaf
abstract: Andy Razaf (born Andriamanantena Paul Razafinkarefo; December 16, 1895 – February 3, 1973) was the American lyricist of such well-known songs as "Ain't Misbehavin'" and "Honeysuckle Rose". He was also a composer, poet and vocalist.

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Now, who was the first person you collaborated with? Andy Razaf. ... during his tenure and mine, we wrote an awful lot of songs together

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Never met Andy Razaf but certainly met his lyrics, I'll tell you that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote a few things, and I used to go over to his house. I would sit down and play the melody note of the things, man, and he could take his pen and just write right along with you

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. No, Andy Razaf was the one -- we were paying a visit to him out here. He lived on Country Club Drive... And he was talking to me and talking to Speedy, you know, and then his wife, and he was telling me about it.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: we was in there recording... Andy Razaf come in with this music under his arm... So we recorded it, and Andy did the vocal on it, too.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Andy Razaf, he wrote them -- most of the music for the shows... Andy Razaf and them, for Tip, Tap, and Toe to use that drum.
